page: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor, utilizing the arm64 architecture. Each core operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, meaning there is no simultaneous multithreading. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific memory and compression tasks. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in PassMark memory tests, supported by uncached large-block memory write speeds of 84.11 GB/sec and cached small-block read speeds of 925.03 GB/sec. Single-threaded performance is notable, with Geekbench single-core scores and text processing benchmarks ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong results. However, cryptographic operations represent a performance tradeoff, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%. Large-model LLM inference also shows limitations, with prompt processing for a 7B model falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated physical cores, the m8g.2xlarge offers a resource-dense profile for general-purpose workloads. The absence of SMT ensures predictable compute performance per vCPU. It is suited for applications such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and text processing. The arm64 architecture and Neoverse-V2 design provide qualitative cost efficiency by delivering competitive memory bandwidth and single-core performance without the overhead of hyperthreading or the premium of x86 licensing.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an arm64-based general-purpose instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. The architecture lacks simultaneous multithreading, ensuring dedicated execution resources per vCPU. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with strong uncached write speeds and single-threaded text processing and compression capabilities. However, cryptographic operations and medium-sized LLM prompt processing represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is designed for balanced workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It offers qualitative cost efficiency through high memory bandwidth and dedicated arm64 cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong memory and compression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 8 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, exceptional memory bandwidth, and balanced resources for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 arm64 physical cores at 2.8 GHz.
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 core-to-RAM ratio.
Top-tier memory performance and strong single-threaded compression speeds.
Tradeoffs in cryptographic operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
